story.iWant: to see branched conversations in visual lanes
story.soThat: I can understand the conversation tree structure at a glance
story.tasks: - Design and create BranchLane component with visual styling - Detect branch points in message tree - Implement single column rendering until branch point - Implement multi-lane layout at branch points using CSS Grid - Implement active branch highlighting - Implement branch collapse/expand functionality - Handle nested branches (up to 2 levels) - Add responsive layout handling - Implement visual connectors between lanes

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Clear visual distinction between lanes (borders, spacing) (TM §5.1)
Active lane highlighted with distinct styling (TM §5.3)
Lanes independently scrollable without affecting other lanes (TM §5.2)
Collapse hides inactive branches, shows collapsed indicator (TM §5.4)
Works with up to 2 levels of nested branches (TM §5.5)
Single column layout until branch point detected (TM §5.2)
Branch indicator visible at fork points

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Lane-based rendering of message branches. Input: Message tree data. Output: React components for lanes. Uses react-window for virtualization.

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Message tree structure with parentMessageId and branchIndex. Root messages: parentMessageId = null. Branch siblings: same parentMessageId, different branchIndex.

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: UI lane rendering: 60fps for up to 3 visible branches simultaneously. Branch visualization renders within 16ms per frame.

artifact.reason: NEW - Main container for lane layout using CSS Grid
NEW - Individual lane component with styling and scroll handling
NEW - Fork point indicator showing branch splits
NEW - Branch point detection logic, scans message tree
NEW - Svelte store tracking active branch per fork point

story-context.constraints: - **Desktop Implementation:** Svelte components for branch visualization. NOT backend work. - **Performance:** Maintain 60fps during lane scrolling. Use CSS Grid for efficient layout. Consider virtualization for long message lists. - **Layout:** Single column until branch detected. Maximum 3 lanes visible (branchIndex 0-9). Nested branches limited to 2 levels for UX clarity. - **Styling:** Lane width 400px, 16px gap, 2px borders. Active lane highlighted with primary color border. Inactive lanes dimmed (opacity 0.6). - **Dependency on E2-S1, E2-S2:** Requires message tree data model and prompt variation flow to create branches to visualize.

interface.name: BranchLane.svelte
interface.signature: Props: {messages: Message[], isActive: boolean, branchIndex: number, onCollapse: () => void}. Renders lane with messages, scroll handling, collapse button.

interface.name: detectBranchPoints()
interface.signature: detectBranchPoints(messages: Message[]): string[] - Returns array of message IDs where branches fork (multiple children with different branchIndex)

interface.name: activeBranchStore
interface.signature: writable<Map<string, number>> - Tracks active branch index per parent message ID

tests.standards: Use vitest for unit tests. Test branch detection algorithm with various tree shapes. Test lane rendering with 1, 2, 3 branches. Test scroll independence between lanes. Test active branch highlighting. Test collapse/expand animations. Visual regression testing for lane layout.

test.idea: Unit test: Render message tree with no branches, verify single column layout (no lanes)
Unit test: Render tree with 1 branch point, verify BranchIndicator shown, 2 lanes rendered
Visual test: Verify lane borders, spacing (400px width, 16px gap, 2px borders)
Unit test: Set active branch index, verify correct lane has highlighted border
Integration test: Scroll lane 1, verify lane 2 position unchanged (independent scroll)
Unit test: Click collapse button, verify lane height transitions to 0, collapsed indicator shown
Integration test: Create tree with 2 levels of branches, verify renders correctly without overflow
Performance test: Measure FPS during lane scroll, verify >= 60fps
